metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a grep oracle to AC3, e.g. `grep -n 'certified PASS' docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit, so the paragraph's key claim is machine-verifiable.
evidence: AC1 oracle: 'grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide" CLAUDE.md'
AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md'
AC4 oracle: 'make lint passes'
AC3 oracle: absent — no grep or other proving command stated
findings.finding: AC3 ('documents one short paragraph on remediation-mode eligibility') has no oracle/proving command, unlike every other AC in the plan. An executing agent has no deterministic in-session check to verify the paragraph was added and contains the required content.
scenarios: An agent writes a paragraph that mentions remediation mode but omits the 'prior certified PASS signature' requirement, and there is no oracle to catch the omission.
A reviewer cannot mechanically verify AC3 is satisfied without reading and judging the prose.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Oracle: `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit inside the new paragraph.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC-4's description of what `make lint` covers for docs-only changes — either note that `make lint` only gates Python (ruff) and the Markdown change passes trivially, or replace the oracle with the actual Markdown formatting gate (e.g. pre-commit `markdownlint` hook if one exists).
evidence: Makefile line 11: `sources = src tests`
Makefile lines 62–64: `lint: ruff check $(sources); ruff format --check $(sources)`
AC-4 text: 'lint covers formatting of any touched files'
Touched files: CLAUDE.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md (both Markdown)
findings.finding: AC-4 claims 'make lint passes (docs changes only; lint covers formatting of any touched files),' but the Makefile's `lint` target runs `ruff check src tests` and `ruff format --check src tests` — it covers Python source only, not Markdown files. The two files being touched (CLAUDE.md, docs/plan-review-gate.md) are Markdown and are not linted by `make lint`.
scenarios: An implementer runs `make lint` expecting it to validate Markdown formatting, but it only checks Python — any Markdown formatting issues in the touched files silently pass the oracle.
AC-4's oracle is effectively a no-op for the actual deliverable files, making it an unverifiable acceptance criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC-4 to: '`make lint` passes (ruff Python-only; Markdown files are not covered by this gate — verify no pre-commit hook rejects the touched .md files).' or check .pre-commit-config.yaml for the actual Markdown gate.

findings.finding: The AC #2 oracle (`grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70) is a negative-only check: it proves the stale text is absent but does not confirm the correct replacement text (the three-tier breakdown with 0.60/0.70/0.75) was actually written. An implementer could delete the stale sentence and pass the oracle without adding any correct threshold documentation.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #2: 'Oracle (positive): `grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit attributing the 0.60 threshold to F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6.'

findings.finding: AC3 has no proving command at all. It requires a paragraph to be added to docs/plan-review-gate.md but provides no oracle or grep to verify the paragraph exists and contains the required content (remediation-mode eligibility, PASS signature prerequisite, cross-reference to floor-eligibility story).
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to AC3:
```
Oracle: `grep -c "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1; `grep -c "remediation-mode eligibility" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns ≥ 1.
```

findings.finding: AC3 (remediation-mode paragraph in docs/plan-review-gate.md) has no verification oracle — the criterion cannot be objectively confirmed as met.
findings.location: AC3: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents (one short paragraph) that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: 'Oracle: `grep -n "certified PASS" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit inside the new paragraph.'

findings.finding: AC2's oracle only proves a negative (0.70 is absent for calibration-2 criteria) but does not prove the positive claim that the correct thresholds (0.60 for F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6; 0.75 for E4) ARE now present in the doc. The criterion says threshold statements 'match src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json' but no proving command validates the correct values exist.
findings.suggested_fix:
Extend AC2's oracle section:
```
# Positive checks
grep -nE '0\.60' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep -E 'F1|E2|G5|T5e|COH|G6'   # must return ≥1 line
grep -nE '0\.75' docs/plan-review-gate.md | grep 'E4'                          # must return ≥1 line
```

findings.finding: AC2's oracle verifies only the ABSENCE of the old wrong threshold (0.70 for calibration-2 criteria) but does not verify PRESENCE of the correct replacement thresholds (0.60, 0.70, 0.75 per group) in the file.
findings.location: AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2 oracle: '`grep -n "0\.60" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6); `grep -n "0\.75" docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns at least one hit (covering E4).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update docs/plan-review-gate.md's line-138 advisory-set description to remove G1G2 and T8 from the 'stays advisory' list and add them (along with T1) to the blocking-criteria enumeration, so the post-fix doc is internally consistent.
evidence: Gate doc line 138: 'Every other LLM criterion stays advisory (0.95), including the demonstrably false-positive-prone set (G1G2/T6/T5b/E5/E6/F4) and the confident-but-routinely-ignored set (T3/T10/T8).'
criteria_routing.json: G1G2 block_threshold=0.70, default_posture=blocking; T8 block_threshold=0.70, default_posture=blocking; T1 block_threshold=0.70, default_posture=blocking
AC-2 oracle only checks that no line describes F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70 — it does not check whether G1G2/T8 are still described as advisory
findings.finding: docs/plan-review-gate.md line 138 also classifies G1G2 as 'demonstrably false-positive-prone' (advisory) and T8/T3/T10 as 'confident-but-routinely-ignored' (advisory), but criteria_routing.json shows G1G2 and T8 are now blocking at 0.70 — the same tier as T1/T4. The plan's AC-2 fix replaces the threshold number at line 135 but leaves this adjacent misclassification text intact, creating an internal contradiction in the post-fix doc: the corrected threshold section will say G1G2/T8 block at 0.70, while line 138 still says they're advisory.
scenarios: After the AC-2 fix, a reader of docs/plan-review-gate.md sees corrected thresholds for F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 but still reads that G1G2 and T8 are advisory — contradicting both the new threshold table AND criteria_routing.json.
An author reading the gate doc to understand which criteria can block their plan would be misled into thinking G1G2 and T8 cannot block them.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise the passage at line 137–139 to reflect the full post-calibration blocking set: G6/COH/T5e/E2/G5/F1 at 0.60, T4/T1/T8/G1G2 at 0.70, E4 at 0.75; and remove G1G2 and T8 from the 'stays advisory' parenthetical.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC1's oracle to scope hits to the target section, e.g. use `awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^##/' CLAUDE.md | grep 'plan-review-criteria-guide'` or equivalent, so the grep only matches within that section.
evidence: AC1 text: 'Oracle: grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide" CLAUDE.md and grep -n "rebar explain" CLAUDE.md each return at least one hit inside that section'
grep -n with no section anchor will match any line in the file, not just lines inside the named section
findings.finding: AC1's oracle (`grep -n 'plan-review-criteria-guide' CLAUDE.md`) does not constrain the hit to the 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section, yet the criterion requires the paragraph to appear 'BEFORE writing a plan' inside that specific section. A false-positive exists if either string appears elsewhere in CLAUDE.md (e.g. an existing reference in a different section).
scenarios: If CLAUDE.md already contains 'plan-review-criteria-guide' in a different section, the oracle passes without the author ever adding the required paragraph to the Ticket template section.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the flat grep with a section-scoped command: `awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## /' CLAUDE.md | grep -c 'plan-review-criteria-guide'` and similarly for 'rebar explain'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the replacement prose for the AC2 section: define what the updated gate-doc text should say (e.g. three threshold groups — 0.60 for F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6; 0.70 for T1/T4/T8/G1G2; 0.75 for E4 — and update 'seven criteria' to accurately reflect the full blocking-tier set), so the implementer has a concrete target beyond the 'absence of 0.70' oracle.
evidence: docs/plan-review-gate.md lines 133-139: 'seven criteria that the verifier rarely refutes … G6, COH, T5e, E2, G5, F1, T4 — are default_posture: blocking at block_threshold: 0.70'
criteria_routing.json: F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 all at block_threshold: 0.6; T1/T4/T8/G1G2 at 0.70; E4 at 0.75
Plan AC2 oracle: 'grep -n "0\.70" docs/plan-review-gate.md shows no line describing F1/E2/G5/T5e/COH/G6 at 0.70' — verifies absence of bad state but does not verify the replacement text is coherent

ABSENCE: no specification in AC2 of what the replacement prose should say (e.g. whether to list three threshold groups, whether to update 'seven criteria' to reflect that T1/T8/G1G2 are now also blocking, what the new framing should be)
findings.finding: AC2 fixes the stale 0.70 threshold for the 0.60-group criteria, but the surrounding gate-doc prose at line 133–135 will remain partially stale: the 'seven criteria' label and the single grouped sentence ('G6, COH, T5e, E2, G5, F1, T4 — are … at block_threshold: 0.70') will need to be restructured into at least two threshold groups (0.60 and 0.70), yet the plan specifies only an oracle for what to remove, not what coherent replacement text to write. The AC also introduces T1/T8/G1G2 (at 0.70 in the JSON) into the gate doc, which currently does not list them as blocking — but the plan is silent on whether the existing gate-doc prose needs expanding to cover these.
scenarios: Implementer removes the '0.70' mention for the 0.60 group but leaves 'seven criteria … G6, COH, T5e, E2, G5, F1, T4 — are blocking at block_threshold: 0.70' partially intact — oracle passes (0.70 now applies only to T4 in that sentence) but the text is now wrong (G6 is in the 0.70 sentence but should be 0.60).
Implementer restructures the prose but omits T1/T8/G1G2 as blocking-tier criteria since the plan doesn't name them in AC2's fix scope, leaving readers unaware of these additional blocking criteria.

findings.finding: AC1's oracle (`grep -n "plan-review-criteria-guide" CLAUDE.md`) searches the entire file but the criterion requires the hit to be INSIDE the 'Ticket template the gates enforce' section. A grep hit outside that section would make the oracle pass while the AC is actually unmet (verify-command defect: substring false-positive / wrong fixture scope).
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the two grep oracles with section-scoped checks:
```
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "plan-review-criteria-guide"
awk '/Ticket template the gates enforce/,/^## [A-Z]/' CLAUDE.md | grep -c "rebar explain"
```
Each must return ≥ 1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise AC3's paragraph specification to accurately describe the actual mechanism: remediation mode cannot engage during BLOCK loops because the code-unchanged precondition compares `verified_at_sha` against the prior signed manifest's SHA — and a BLOCK produces no signed manifest, so there is no baseline SHA to compare (not because there is an explicit PASS-signature eligibility check).
evidence: Plan AC3: 'remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature, so the rising floor does not engage during BLOCK loops'

docs/plan-review-gate.md lines 323-325: 'the code is unchanged since the baseline (detected by verified_at_sha equality against the prior signed manifest — reusing the signed snapshot ref, no new diff machinery)'

ec89 ticket description: 'code UNCHANGED since baseline — detected deterministically by comparing the gate's attested verified_at_sha (the origin/main snapshot SHA the gate signs into the prior review's manifest) against the current run's verified_at_sha. Equal → code unchanged; differ → code drifted.'

ABSENCE: no explicit 'check for a prior PASS signature before enabling remediation mode' precondition exists in the gate code or docs; the effect is a byproduct of the code-unchanged precondition requiring a baseline SHA from a prior signed manifest
findings.finding: AC3 documents remediation-mode eligibility as 'requires a prior certified PASS signature,' but the actual gate mechanism is that the code-unchanged precondition compares `verified_at_sha` against the prior *signed manifest's* SHA — so remediation mode cannot engage during BLOCK loops not because of an explicit PASS-signature check, but because no prior signed manifest means no baseline SHA to compare. Documenting the wrong causal mechanism produces a misleading 'honest current behavior' paragraph.
scenarios: A reader of the new paragraph concludes there is an explicit eligibility check for a PASS signature, and modifies the gate expecting to unlock remediation mode during BLOCK loops by changing that check — but the real gate is the missing verified_at_sha from the absent manifest.
A future maintainer reads 'requires a prior certified PASS signature' and doesn't understand why removing the PASS requirement wouldn't enable rising-floor convergence during BLOCK loops.
findings.suggested_fix: State the mechanism accurately: 'Remediation-mode eligibility requires a prior `verified_at_sha` baseline, which is only available when a prior PASS signed a manifest. Since a BLOCK produces no signed manifest, the rising floor's code-unchanged precondition has no baseline SHA to compare and falls back to a full review — the rising floor never engages during BLOCK loops as a byproduct of this missing baseline, not via an explicit PASS-signature check.'

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the floor-eligibility story's ticket ID (e.g. `ec89`) in AC#3 so an executing agent knows exactly which story to cross-reference in the new `docs/plan-review-gate.md` paragraph.
evidence: AC#3 text: 'cross-referencing the floor-eligibility story in this epic'
Repo-wide search for 'floor-eligibility' returns zero hits in any source file
docs/adr/0008-convergent-plan-edit-re-review.md references child 'ec89' as the remediation-mode eligibility story, but the plan does not cite this ID
An executing agent must write prose cross-referencing a ticket it cannot identify from the plan text
findings.finding: AC#3 says the new paragraph should cross-reference 'the floor-eligibility story in this epic,' but the plan never names this story's ticket ID, and no file in the repo contains the literal phrase 'floor-eligibility story.' The ADR 0008 mentions a story 'ec89' (remediation-mode eligibility) as the relevant child, but the plan leaves an executing agent unable to find or link to the correct story without guessing — the cross-reference is an unbacked forward pointer.
scenarios: An agent implementing AC#3 cannot determine which story to link/cite in the new paragraph — it may link the wrong ticket or omit the link entirely, making the cross-reference vacuous.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'cross-referencing the floor-eligibility story in this epic' with 'cross-referencing story `ec89` (remediation-mode eligibility, epic 7d43)' in AC#3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the plan description (or a brief 'Scope' subsection) naming the third deliverable: documenting remediation-mode eligibility behavior in plan-review-gate.md, so AC3 has covering described work.
evidence: Plan description: 'Route the existing criterion->authoring mapping to plan authors, and fix the gate doc's stale threshold numbers.'
AC3: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md documents (one short paragraph) that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature...'
ABSENCE: no mention of 'remediation-mode', 'floor-eligibility', or 'BLOCK loops' anywhere in the plan body outside AC3.
findings.finding: AC3 (remediation-mode eligibility paragraph in plan-review-gate.md) covers a deliverable that is entirely absent from the plan's description/title. The plan body mentions only two workstreams: routing authoring guidance and fixing stale threshold numbers. A third workstream — documenting remediation-mode eligibility — has no corresponding description, making AC3 an orphan criterion with no covering work narrative.
scenarios: An implementer reading only the plan description would not know to write the remediation-mode paragraph, leaving AC3 unsatisfied.
A reviewer auditing scope would flag AC3 as out-of-scope scope creep not explained by the stated problem.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the plan description to: '…and fix the gate doc's stale threshold numbers. Also add a note clarifying that remediation-mode eligibility currently requires a prior certified PASS signature, so the rising score floor does not engage during BLOCK loops.'
